faqs.question: What causes drain fly infestations in South Ozone Park homes?
faqs.answer: Drain flies breed in the organic matter that accumulates inside drains, pipes, and sewage systems. In South Ozone Park, older plumbing, inconsistent drain maintenance, and humid conditions create ideal breeding environments. These small flies are often found near kitchen sinks, bathroom drains, and basement floor drains where moisture and organic buildup occur.

faqs.question: How long does drain fly treatment take in South Ozone Park?
faqs.answer: Most drain fly treatments in South Ozone Park take 1-2 hours depending on the severity of the infestation and the number of affected drains. Our technicians thoroughly inspect all potential breeding sites, apply bio-enzymatic treatments to break down organic matter, and may use additional methods like foam or gel treatments for stubborn cases. Follow-up visits may be recommended for severe infestations.

faqs.question: What's included in the $350-$550 drain fly treatment price?
faqs.answer: Our drain fly treatment includes a complete property inspection, identification of all breeding sources, professional-grade bio-enzymatic drain treatments, mechanical cleaning when necessary, application of residual products, and detailed prevention recommendations. The price varies based on property size, number of drains affected, and infestation severity. We provide upfront pricing before starting any work.

faqs.question: How can I prevent drain flies from returning after treatment?
faqs.answer: Prevent drain fly recurrence by regularly cleaning drains with hot water and brushes, using monthly bio-enzymatic drain cleaners, fixing any leaky pipes or faucets, ensuring proper ventilation in bathrooms and kitchens, and avoiding standing water. We provide specific maintenance guidelines for your South Ozone Park property and offer follow-up treatments if needed to ensure complete elimination.
